## File: `000_OPERATOR_README.md`

# Autonomous Marketing Agent — Markdown-Only Operating Pack

This pack is the corrected direction: agent-native, Markdown-first, tactics-heavy, and built for Claude Code/Codex-style operation.

It keeps the prior research and autonomous ad factory material, but adds an explicit operating stance:

> Do not build a committee. Build a self-running marketing agent repo.

The agent should read Markdown, create Markdown, update Markdown memory, and run through a CLI harness. The core unit is not a meeting or approval flow. The core unit is a Markdown artifact: tactic cards, buyer-state cards, claim/fact cards, experiment cards, platform payload notes, campaign run logs, and learning memos.

## Priority order

When using this pack in an agent harness, load these first:

1. `AGENTS.md`
2. `CLAUDE.md` or `CODEX.md`
3. `docs/no_committee_autonomous_marketing_model.md`
4. `docs/markdown_memory_architecture.md`
5. `docs/campaign_autopilot_loop.md`
6. `docs/marketing_agent_roles.md`
7. `docs/tactic_library_index.md`
8. relevant skills under `skills/`
9. relevant tactic cards under `tactics/`
10. logs and templates under `templates/`

## Operating law

Autonomous marketing is not an AI that randomly invents ads.

Autonomous marketing is a closed-loop system that:

1. reads product facts, market evidence, buyer language, and performance logs;
2. chooses a strategy and tactic;
3. generates assets;
4. produces launch-ready platform payload notes;
5. records what it did;
6. measures what happened;
7. updates the tactic and learning memory.

## Everything Markdown

This archive intentionally contains only Markdown files. Former CSV, JSON, YAML, SQL, HTML, TOML, BibTeX, Python, and text files from the previous pack have been converted into Markdown wrappers or Markdown tables.

## Important distinction

This is not a governance pack. It is a harness pack.

- Governance mindset: meetings, committees, slow review.
- Harness mindset: repo instructions, skills, facts, budgets, run logs, platform constraints, and automatic self-checks.

The agent should run hard inside the harness.

---

## File: `01_six_month_curriculum.md`

# 24-Week Curriculum
The plan assumes about 5-7 focused hours per week: 3-4 hours reading, 1-2 hours notes, 1-2 hours practical application.

## Month 1 - Advertising reality and skeptical buyers
### Week 1: Install the base model: ads do not create belief; they earn inspection
**Required reading**
- [How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know?](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103) - Demetrios Vakratsas; Tim Ambler (1999). _Peer-reviewed review paper_. Bias note: Academic review; older but foundational; not platform-funded
- [A Behavior Model for Persuasive Design](https://www.demenzemedicinagenerale.net/images/mens-sana/Captology_Fogg_Behavior_Model.pdf) - B. J. Fogg (2009). _Academic conference paper_. Bias note: Persuasive design lens; can be misused if not ethically constrained
- [The Persuasion Knowledge Model: How People Cope with Persuasion Attempts](https://academic.oup.com/jcr/article/21/1/1/1853712) - Marian Friestad; Peter Wright (1994). _Peer-reviewed theory paper_. Bias note: Theory paper; requires application judgment

**Output:** Write a 1-page model of how a skeptical buyer moves from “this is an ad” to “this is worth checking”.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 2: Advertising effectiveness: what is real, what is exaggerated
**Required reading**
- [How Well Does Advertising Work? Generalizations from Meta-Analysis of Brand Advertising Elasticities](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1509/jmkr.48.3.457) - Raj Sethuraman; Gerard J. Tellis; Richard A. Briesch (2011). _Peer-reviewed meta-analysis_. Bias note: Academic meta-analysis; publication and method heterogeneity still matter
- [Meta-analysis of Advertising Effectiveness: New Insights from Improved Bias Corrections](https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf) - Joseph Korkames; T. D. Stanley; Stefan Stremersch (2025/2026). _Peer-reviewed meta-analysis / bias correction_. Bias note: Academic; explicitly focused on correcting publication/misspecification/aggregation bias
- [Does Advertising Work? Evidence from the Past and Some New Evidence](https://www.gc.cuny.edu/sites/default/files/2021-07/Does-Avertising-Work--Winer.pdf) - Russ Winer (2012). _Academic presentation / review_. Bias note: Lecture deck; interpret as synthesis, not primary evidence

**Output:** Create a “marketing claims I will no longer believe without evidence” checklist.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 3: Short-term activation vs long-term brand building
**Required reading**
- [The Long and the Short of It: Balancing Short and Long-Term Marketing Strategies](https://ipa.co.uk/knowledge/publications-reports/the-long-and-the-short-of-it-balancing-short-and-long-term-marketing-strategies) - Les Binet; Peter Field (2013). _Practitioner/industry effectiveness analysis_. Bias note: Practitioner and award-case database; useful but not neutral academic evidence
- [How Brands Grow](https://marketingscience.info/news-and-insights/grow-brand-grow) - Byron Sharp (2010). _Book / evidence-based marketing_. Bias note: Ehrenberg-Bass school has strong viewpoint; compare with segmentation/positioning literature
- [The DNA of Distinctive Brands: Guide to Distinctive Brand Assets](https://www.distinctivebat.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/The-DNA-of-Distinctive-Brands-Distinctive-BAT-Guide-To-DBAs.pdf) - Distinctive BAT (2024). _Practitioner guide_. Bias note: Commercial consultancy; useful framework but verify with independent evidence

**Output:** Choose 5 software brands and identify their mental availability cues and distinctive assets.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 4: Ad avoidance and attention filters
**Required reading**
- [Banner Blindness: Web Searchers Often Miss “Obvious” Links](https://www.ruf.rice.edu/~lane/papers/banner_blindness.pdf) - Jan Panero Benway; David M. Lane (1998/2007). _Usability research paper_. Bias note: Old web context but human attention pattern remains useful
- [Banner Blindness Revisited: Users Dodge Ads on Mobile and Desktop](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-old-and-new-findings/) - Nielsen Norman Group (2018). _UX research article_. Bias note: UX consultancy; credible but commercial
- [Banner Blindness: The Original Eyetracking Research](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-original-eyetracking/) - Nielsen Norman Group (2007). _UX research article_. Bias note: UX consultancy; interpret alongside academic work
- [Ad Skepticism: The Consequences of Disbelief](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00913367.2005.10639199) - Carl Obermiller; Eric Spangenberg; Douglas L. MacLachlan (2005). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Academic; specific measures of skepticism

**Output:** Build a table of ad patterns that trigger your own skepticism vs patterns that earn inspection.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?

## Month 2 - Buyer psychology and trust
### Week 5: Persuasion theory without manipulation
**Required reading**
- [The Elaboration Likelihood Model of Persuasion](https://richardepetty.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/1986-advances-pettycacioppo.pdf) - Richard E. Petty; John T. Cacioppo (1986). _Foundational persuasion theory chapter_. Bias note: Classic model; not a conversion playbook by itself
- [A Behavior Model for Persuasive Design](https://www.demenzemedicinagenerale.net/images/mens-sana/Captology_Fogg_Behavior_Model.pdf) - B. J. Fogg (2009). _Academic conference paper_. Bias note: Persuasive design lens; can be misused if not ethically constrained
- [The Fogg Behavior Model](https://behaviordesign.stanford.edu/resources/fogg-behavior-model) - Stanford Behavior Design Lab (Current). _Official framework page_. Bias note: Official model page; simplified

**Output:** Map five ads into motivation, ability, prompt, and central/peripheral processing.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 6: Software adoption psychology
**Required reading**
- [Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Ease of Use, and User Acceptance of Information Technology](https://www.jstor.org/stable/249008) - Fred D. Davis (1989). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Classic model; later models add context, norms, and habit
- [Technology Acceptance Model Overview](https://open.ncl.ac.uk/theory-library/technology-acceptance-model.pdf) - TheoryHub / Newcastle University (2025). _Theory overview_. Bias note: Secondary summary
- [Detailed Review of Rogers' Diffusion of Innovations Theory](https://tilisathibodeaux.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Sahin.pdf) - Ismail Sahin (2006). _Academic review paper_. Bias note: Secondary review of Rogers; education-tech angle

**Output:** For one software product, write perceived usefulness and perceived ease-of-use proof points.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 7: Trust, proof, reviews, and source credibility
**Required reading**
- [Beyond Martech: Building Trust with Consumers and Engaging Where Sentiment Is High](https://www.nielsen.com/insights/2021/beyond-martech-building-trust-with-consumers-and-engaging-where-sentiment-is-high/) - Nielsen (2021). _Industry research / trust in advertising_. Bias note: Measurement company; still useful for channel-trust comparisons
- [The Effect of Characteristics of Source Credibility on Consumer Behaviour: A Meta-Analysis](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698918307926) - Elvira Ismagilova et al. (2020). _Peer-reviewed meta-analysis_. Bias note: Academic meta-analysis; mostly eWOM contexts
- [How Online Reviews Affect Purchase Intention: A Meta-Analysis Across Contextual and Cultural Factors](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2543925123000323) - Keda Qiu; Liyi Zhang (2024). _Peer-reviewed meta-analysis_. Bias note: Review literature heterogeneity; still strong source
- [Meta-Analytic Review of Online Purchase Intention](https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/325948/1/10.1080_23311975.2023.2296686.pdf) - Moumita Ghosh et al. (2024). _Peer-reviewed meta-analysis_. Bias note: Broad e-commerce scope; not SaaS-specific

**Output:** Design a proof stack for a skeptical buyer: peer proof, expert proof, product proof, risk reversal.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 8: Jobs-to-be-Done and customer interviews
**Required reading**
- [Know Your Customers' Jobs to Be Done](https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done) - Clayton M. Christensen; Taddy Hall; Karen Dillon; David S. Duncan (2016). _HBR article / strategy framework_. Bias note: Practitioner/consulting style; use with interviews, not slogans
- [The Consumer Decision Journey](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-consumer-decision-journey) - David Court; Dave Elzinga; Susan Mulder; Ole Jørgen Vetvik (2009). _Consulting/practitioner model_. Bias note: Consulting framework; not peer-reviewed
- [The New Consumer Decision Journey](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-new-consumer-decision-journey) - McKinsey (2015). _Consulting/practitioner model_. Bias note: Consulting perspective

**Output:** Run 3 mock JTBD interviews or analyze 3 user reviews to infer the “job”.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?

## Month 3 - Measurement and experimentation
### Week 9: Digital ad measurement basics
**Required reading**
- [Online Advertising](https://www-2.rotman.utoronto.ca/~agoldfarb/OnlineAdvertising.pdf) - Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker (2011/2014). _Academic handbook chapter_. Bias note: Academic overview; older but still foundational
- [Measuring the Effects of Advertising: The Digital Frontier](https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w19520/w19520.pdf) - Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao; David H. Reiley (2013/2015). _NBER/academic chapter_. Bias note: Authors have worked at ad platforms; still one of the strongest measurement papers
- [Online Display Advertising Markets: A Literature Review and Future Directions](https://hanachoi.github.io/research-papers/choi_et_al_review_display_advertising.pdf) - Hana Choi; Carl Mela; Santiago R. Balseiro; Adam Leary (2019). _Academic literature review_. Bias note: Academic review; technical

**Output:** Draw the difference between click attribution, conversion attribution, incrementality, and profit.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 10: Incrementality and why dashboards lie
**Required reading**
- [The Unfavorable Economics of Measuring the Returns to Advertising](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2367103) - Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao (2015). _Peer-reviewed field experiment evidence_. Bias note: Strong empirical design; not a “marketing is useless” paper, a measurement precision paper
- [A Comparison of Approaches to Advertising Measurement: Evidence from Big Field Experiments at Facebook](https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf) - Brett R. Gordon; Florian Zettelmeyer; Neha Bhargava; Daniel Chapsky (2018/2019). _Peer-reviewed field experiment comparison_. Bias note: Facebook experiment data; platform context matters, but methodology lessons are broad
- [Consumer Heterogeneity and Paid Search Effectiveness: A Large-Scale Field Experiment](https://faculty.haas.berkeley.edu/stadelis/BNT_ECMA_rev.pdf) - Thomas Blake; Chris Nosko; Steven Tadelis (2015). _Peer-reviewed field experiment_. Bias note: eBay context; generalize carefully

**Output:** Write rules for when you trust ROAS, when you distrust it, and what test would convince you.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 11: Field experiments and A/B testing discipline
**Required reading**
- [Online Controlled Experiments at Large Scale](https://exp-platform.com/Documents/2013%20controlledExperimentsAtScale.pdf) - Ron Kohavi et al. (2013). _Academic/practitioner paper_. Bias note: Written by experimentation practitioners; highly practical
- [Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments: A Practical Guide to A/B Testing](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/trustworthy-online-controlled-experiments/4EC18E75D4F1D3A576B28E0CBD49F5B9) - Ron Kohavi; Diane Tang; Ya Xu (2020). _Book_. Bias note: Practitioner-led but rigorous; book is not free in full
- [Unexpected Results in Online Controlled Experiments](https://kdd.org/exploration_files/v12-02-8-UR-Kohavi.pdf) - Ron Kohavi; Roger Longbotham (2010). _Academic/practitioner paper_. Bias note: Practitioner perspective; valuable failure modes

**Output:** Draft an experiment plan for a landing-page or onboarding change, including success metric and guardrails.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 12: Display/search effects and non-click impact
**Required reading**
- [The Online Display Ad Effectiveness Funnel & Carryover: Lessons from 432 Field Experiments](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Johnson-Garrett-PAPER-VERSION-2.pdf) - Garrett A. Johnson; Randall A. Lewis; Elmar I. Nubbemeyer (2017). _Field experiment meta-study_. Bias note: Display-ad platform setting; still valuable scale and causal design
- [Online Ads and Offline Sales: Measuring the Effects of Retail Advertising via a Controlled Experiment on Yahoo!](https://www.davidreiley.com/papers/OnlineAdsOfflineSales.pdf) - Randall A. Lewis; David H. Reiley (2014). _Peer-reviewed controlled experiment_. Bias note: Retail context; still useful for click vs non-click impact
- [Privacy Regulation and Online Advertising](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Privacy-Regulation-and-Online-Advertising.pdf) - Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker (2011). _Peer-reviewed research paper_. Bias note: Academic; context predates today’s privacy stack but mechanisms matter

**Output:** Create a “buyer actions before/after click” map: impressions, searches, direct visits, referrals, trials.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?

## Month 4 - SaaS/product-led/B2B buying
### Week 13: B2B/SaaS buying journey
**Required reading**
- [2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report](https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf) - G2 (2025). _Vendor/industry report_. Bias note: Review marketplace with commercial incentive; useful current signal
- [B2B Tech Buying in the Age of AI](https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-Bridging-the-Trust-Gap-B2B-Tech-Buying-in-the-Age-of-AI.pdf) - TrustRadius (2025). _Vendor/industry report_. Bias note: Review marketplace with commercial incentive; still useful buyer survey data
- [Gartner Sales Survey Finds 61% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Buying Experience](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2025-06-25-gartner-sales-survey-finds-61-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-buying-experience) - Gartner (2025). _Analyst research press release_. Bias note: Analyst firm; full methodology may be gated
- [How B2B SaaS Marketing Leaders Buy Software in 2024](https://info.wynter.com/hubfs/B2B%20buying%20journey%20study%20by%20Wynter.pdf) - Wynter (2024). _Vendor research report_. Bias note: Wynter sells message testing; small sample; still very tactical

**Output:** Build a B2B self-serve buying checklist: pricing, docs, demo, security, integrations, reviews, comparisons.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 14: Free trials, freemium, activation
**Required reading**
- [Converting Free Users to Paid Subscribers in the SaaS Context](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3945909) - Hongshuang (Alice) Li (2022). _Peer-reviewed SaaS marketing paper_. Bias note: Single SaaS-firm data; excellent relevance, limited generality
- [Longer or Shorter? A Large-Scale Randomized Field Experiment on Free Trial Duration in the Freemium Model](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12217587/) - Ling Zhang; Jiang Duan (2025). _Open-access field experiment_. Bias note: One SaaS company; still valuable because randomized and large-scale
- [Freemium Pricing: Evidence from a Large-Scale Field Experiment](https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/155324/1/880313099.pdf) - Julian Runge et al. (2016). _Working paper / field experiment_. Bias note: Game/software context; use as freemium mechanics evidence

**Output:** Design a trial-to-paid lifecycle with activation events, emails, in-app prompts, and risk reversal.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 15: The zero-price trap and product-led mechanics
**Required reading**
- [The Zero-Price Effect in Freemium Business Models](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/mar.21211) - Thomas Niemand et al. (2019). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Behavioral experiments; not a complete GTM model
- [Designing Freemium with Usage Limitation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1567422322000370) - Takumi Kato (2022). _Peer-reviewed modeling paper_. Bias note: Model-based; validate against real product data
- [The SaaS Conversion Report](https://chartmogul.com/reports/saas-conversion-report-2/) - ChartMogul (Current). _Vendor/industry benchmark_. Bias note: Subscription analytics vendor; benchmarks are directional
- [Product Benchmarks / Product-Led Growth Benchmarks](https://openviewpartners.com/benchmarks/) - OpenView (Current). _VC/practitioner benchmark_. Bias note: VC lens; data set selection and incentives matter

**Output:** Decide what a free tier should limit: usage, seats, integrations, exports, automation, support, or data retention.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 16: Product demos and buyer self-education
**Required reading**
- [2025 Buyer Behavior Report](https://goconsensus.com/research/2025-b2b-buyer-behavior-report) - Consensus (2025). _Vendor/industry report_. Bias note: Interactive demo vendor; may overstate demo/product-tour value
- [Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience) - Gartner (2026). _Analyst research press release_. Bias note: Analyst firm; current but press-release level
- [How B2B SaaS Marketing Leaders Buy Software in 2024](https://info.wynter.com/hubfs/B2B%20buying%20journey%20study%20by%20Wynter.pdf) - Wynter (2024). _Vendor research report_. Bias note: Wynter sells message testing; small sample; still very tactical

**Output:** Create a demo outline that makes a skeptical buyer say “show me” without needing sales pressure.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?

## Month 5 - Pricing, UX, ethics, and trust
### Week 17: Pricing psychology and transparency
**Required reading**
- [Consumer Reactions to Drip Pricing](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Morwitz-Vicki-Wroe-Alderson-PAPER-2020.pdf) - S. Santana; S. Dallas; V. G. Morwitz (2020). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Specific pricing mechanism; important ethical boundary
- [Consumers' Reactions to Partitioned Prices](https://www.jstor.org/stable/pdf/3152164.pdf) - Vicki G. Morwitz; Eric A. Greenleaf; Eric J. Johnson (1998). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Classic paper; older and often misused by dark-pattern marketers
- [A Review of Research on Partitioned Pricing](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S105774081500042X) - Eric A. Greenleaf et al. (2016). _Peer-reviewed literature review_. Bias note: Review literature; not SaaS-specific
- [Behavioral Response to Price: Data-Based Insights and Future Research Directions](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0022435922000124) - Gurumurthy Kalyanaram et al. (2022). _Peer-reviewed review_. Bias note: Retail-oriented pricing literature

**Output:** Audit 10 SaaS pricing pages for clarity, anchoring, hidden costs, and buyer anxiety.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 18: Website credibility and landing-page trust
**Required reading**
- [Trustworthiness in Web Design: 4 Credibility Factors](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/trustworthy-design/) - Nielsen Norman Group (2016). _UX research article_. Bias note: UX consultancy; practical rather than academic
- [Ecommerce Usability Improvements](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ecommerce-improvements/) - Nielsen Norman Group (2011). _UX research article_. Bias note: E-commerce focus; translate carefully to SaaS
- [Ecommerce User Experience: Trust and Credibility](https://www.nngroup.com/reports/ecommerce-ux-trust-and-credibility/) - Nielsen Norman Group (Current edition). _Paid UX report_. Bias note: Paid consultancy report; valuable if accessible
- [Checkout UX / Ecommerce UX Research](https://baymard.com/research) - Baymard Institute (Current). _UX benchmark/research_. Bias note: UX research company; credible but commercial

**Output:** Teardown 5 SaaS landing pages and score them on clarity, proof, friction, specificity, and credibility.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 19: Personalization, privacy, and trust backlash
**Required reading**
- [Unraveling the Personalization Paradox](https://cris.maastrichtuniversity.nl/ws/files/65443620/mahr_2015_unraveling_the_personalization_paradox.pdf) - Elizabeth Aguirre; Dominik Mahr; Dhruv Grewal; Ko de Ruyter; Martin Wetzels (2015). _Peer-reviewed paper_. Bias note: Experimental settings; implications depend on current privacy norms
- [Privacy Regulation and Online Advertising](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Privacy-Regulation-and-Online-Advertising.pdf) - Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker (2011). _Peer-reviewed research paper_. Bias note: Academic; context predates today’s privacy stack but mechanisms matter
- [2025 Edelman Trust Barometer Special Report: Brand Trust, From We to Me](https://www.edelman.com/sites/g/files/aatuss191/files/2025-06/2025%20Edelman%20Trust%20Barometer%20Special%20Report%20Brand%20Trust%2C%20From%20We%20to%20Me_FINAL_0.pdf) - Edelman (2025). _Industry trust report_. Bias note: PR firm; trust framing can serve client interests

**Output:** Write rules for ethical personalization: what is helpful, what is creepy, what needs disclosure.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 20: Dark patterns and ethical boundaries
**Required reading**
- [Bringing Dark Patterns to Light](https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/ftc_gov/pdf/P214800%2BDark%2BPatterns%2BReport%2B9.14.2022%2B-%2BFINAL.pdf) - U.S. Federal Trade Commission (2022). _Government/regulator report_. Bias note: Regulator lens; emphasizes consumer harm
- [Dark Commercial Patterns](https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/dark-commercial-patterns_44f5e846-en.html) - OECD (2022). _Intergovernmental evidence report_. Bias note: Policy/regulator lens; not a growth handbook
- [Online Choice Architecture: How Digital Design Can Harm Competition and Consumers](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/online-choice-architecture-how-digital-design-can-harm-competition-and-consumers) - UK Competition and Markets Authority (2022). _Regulator discussion paper_. Bias note: Competition/consumer protection lens
- [Dark Patterns at Scale: Findings from a Crawl of 11K Shopping Websites](https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.07032) - Arunesh Mathur et al. (2019). _Peer-reviewed HCI paper_. Bias note: Shopping-site focus; taxonomy useful across digital products

**Output:** Create a red-line list: tactics you refuse to use, safer alternatives, and how to measure harm.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?

## Month 6 - Synthesis and build
### Week 21: Online choice architecture and friction design
**Required reading**
- [Evidence Review of Online Choice Architecture and Consumer and Competition Harm](https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6258499fd3bf7f600e76d9de/OCA_Evidence_Review_Paper_14.4.22.pdf) - UK CMA (2022). _Regulator evidence review_. Bias note: Regulator/evidence review; deep but harm-oriented
- [Sludge Audits](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/behavioural-public-policy/article/sludge-audits/12A7E338984CE8807CC1E078EC4F13A7) - Cass R. Sunstein (2022). _Peer-reviewed public policy paper_. Bias note: Policy perspective; useful ethical lens
- [Fixing Frictions: Sludge Audits Around the World](https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2024/06/fixing-frictions-sludge-audits-around-the-world_1b4bbf1a/5e9bb35c-en.pdf) - OECD (2024). _Government/intergovernmental report_. Bias note: Public-sector lens; not commercial conversion-focused
- [ICPEN Dark Patterns in Subscription Services Sweep Public Report](https://www.oaic.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0018/232623/Public-Report-ICPEN-Dark-Patterns-Sweep.pdf) - ICPEN consumer protection agencies (2024). _International regulator sweep report_. Bias note: Regulator lens; subscription focus

**Output:** Run a sludge audit on a signup, billing, or cancellation flow.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 22: Channel strategy and discovery
**Required reading**
- [Digital 2025: How People Discover New Brands](https://datareportal.com/reports/digital-2025-sub-section-brand-discovery) - DataReportal / GWI (2025). _Global digital behavior report_. Bias note: Industry report using survey data; good macro directional signal
- [Digital 2025: Global Overview Report](https://datareportal.com/reports/digital-2025-global-overview-report) - DataReportal / We Are Social / Meltwater (2025). _Global digital report_. Bias note: Industry synthesis; check methodology
- [Media Reactions 2025](https://www.kantar.com/campaigns/media-reactions) - Kantar (2025). _Industry research report_. Bias note: Research vendor; commercial but useful for ad receptivity trends
- [Trust in Advertising Study](https://www.nielsen.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2022/02/2021-Nielsen-Trust-In-Advertising-U.S.-sell-sheet.pdf) - Nielsen (2021). _Industry research sell sheet_. Bias note: Research product sell sheet; limited detail

**Output:** Build a channel map for one software product: search, reviews, content, social, community, referrals, paid.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 23: Case study teardown sprint
**Required reading**
- [How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know?](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103) - Demetrios Vakratsas; Tim Ambler (1999). _Peer-reviewed review paper_. Bias note: Academic review; older but foundational; not platform-funded
- [Converting Free Users to Paid Subscribers in the SaaS Context](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3945909) - Hongshuang (Alice) Li (2022). _Peer-reviewed SaaS marketing paper_. Bias note: Single SaaS-firm data; excellent relevance, limited generality
- [2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report](https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf) - G2 (2025). _Vendor/industry report_. Bias note: Review marketplace with commercial incentive; useful current signal
- [Trustworthiness in Web Design: 4 Credibility Factors](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/trustworthy-design/) - Nielsen Norman Group (2016). _UX research article_. Bias note: UX consultancy; practical rather than academic

**Output:** Teardown 3 full funnels: ad -> landing page -> signup -> activation -> upgrade.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?
### Week 24: Capstone: build your own ethical persuasion system
**Required reading**
- [Bringing Dark Patterns to Light](https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/ftc_gov/pdf/P214800%2BDark%2BPatterns%2BReport%2B9.14.2022%2B-%2BFINAL.pdf) - U.S. Federal Trade Commission (2022). _Government/regulator report_. Bias note: Regulator lens; emphasizes consumer harm
- [A Comparison of Approaches to Advertising Measurement: Evidence from Big Field Experiments at Facebook](https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf) - Brett R. Gordon; Florian Zettelmeyer; Neha Bhargava; Daniel Chapsky (2018/2019). _Peer-reviewed field experiment comparison_. Bias note: Facebook experiment data; platform context matters, but methodology lessons are broad
- [Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments: A Practical Guide to A/B Testing](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/trustworthy-online-controlled-experiments/4EC18E75D4F1D3A576B28E0CBD49F5B9) - Ron Kohavi; Diane Tang; Ya Xu (2020). _Book_. Bias note: Practitioner-led but rigorous; book is not free in full
- [Know Your Customers' Jobs to Be Done](https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done) - Clayton M. Christensen; Taddy Hall; Karen Dillon; David S. Duncan (2016). _HBR article / strategy framework_. Bias note: Practitioner/consulting style; use with interviews, not slogans

**Output:** Produce a complete GTM brief for a software product: persona, job, messaging, proof, funnel, experiment plan, ethics audit.

**Operating question:** What would make a skeptical, competent buyer inspect further without feeling pressured?


---

# Methods Overlay for the 24-Week Plan

Use this overlay alongside the weekly reading. Each month should add methods, not just concepts.

## Month 1 method focus: skepticism and proof paths

Study:
- Product demonstration ads
- Interactive demo / sandbox
- Comparison pages
- Reviews/testimonials
- Risk reversal

Output: redesign 5 ads so they do not ask for belief. Each should route to an evidence path.

## Month 2 method focus: demand capture and measurement

Study:
- High-intent search ads
- SEO problem-content
- Marketplace listings
- A/B testing and incrementality testing

Output: create one demand-capture plan with hypothesis, landing page, primary metric, guardrail metric, and measurement caveats.

## Month 3 method focus: trust and buyer psychology

Study:
- Case studies
- Source credibility
- Reviews/testimonials
- Referral systems
- Community-led growth

Output: design a trust system for one software product: proof assets, independent evidence, review strategy, and ethical boundaries.

## Month 4 method focus: SaaS/product-led conversion

Study:
- Free trial
- Freemium
- Product-led onboarding
- Lifecycle email/in-app nurture
- Pricing and packaging

Output: build a trial-to-paid conversion map showing activation event, first-value path, upgrade trigger, and churn risk.

## Month 5 method focus: complex buying and distribution

Study:
- Sales-assisted demo/discovery
- Account-based marketing
- Outbound email/LinkedIn
- Webinars/workshops
- Partner/integration co-marketing

Output: create an enterprise buying pack with stakeholder map, proof path, risk objections, and sales-assisted demo structure.

## Month 6 method focus: integrated GTM system

Study:
- Customer success as marketing
- Expansion/upsell
- Reactivation/winback
- Launch campaigns/beta programs
- Ethics audit

Output: final capstone method stack:
- 1 demand creation method
- 1 demand capture method
- 1 proof/risk reduction method
- 1 product-led activation method
- 1 retention/expansion method
- 1 measurement plan
- 1 ethics audit

# Generative AI overlay

The original 24-week curriculum remains the backbone. Add `29_six_month_ai_overlay_curriculum.md` as a parallel track.

The AI overlay should be used in this order:

1. **Weeks 1-4:** AI fundamentals, skepticism, governance, and source discipline.
2. **Weeks 5-8:** AI-assisted research, ICP, JTBD, positioning, and proof architecture.
3. **Weeks 9-12:** AI-assisted creative, landing pages, emails, and experiment design.
4. **Weeks 13-16:** AI for SaaS sales, onboarding, customer success, and expansion.
5. **Weeks 17-20:** AI search, GEO, answer engines, buyer agents, and agentic commerce.
6. **Weeks 21-24:** AI governance, measurement, capstone build, and final GTM system.

The rule is not "use AI every week." The rule is: learn where AI gives leverage and where it creates risk.

---

## File: `02_annotated_bibliography.md`

# Annotated Bibliography
Sources are grouped by category. The `Bias note` is not a dismissal; it tells you how to use the source without being captured by it.

## Advertising effectiveness
### How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know?
- **Authors/org:** Demetrios Vakratsas; Tim Ambler
- **Year:** 1999
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed review paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher/JSTOR page; library access may be needed
- **Link:** https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103
- **Why read:** Breaks the simplistic ad -> belief -> purchase model; maps cognition, affect, and experience effects.
- **Bias note:** Academic review; older but foundational; not platform-funded
### How Well Does Advertising Work? Generalizations from Meta-Analysis of Brand Advertising Elasticities
- **Authors/org:** Raj Sethuraman; Gerard J. Tellis; Richard A. Briesch
- **Year:** 2011
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher page; author copies/mirrors may exist
- **Link:** https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1509/jmkr.48.3.457
- **Why read:** Gives empirical benchmarks for how much advertising tends to move sales; prevents magical thinking.
- **Bias note:** Academic meta-analysis; publication and method heterogeneity still matter
### Meta-analysis of Advertising Effectiveness: New Insights from Improved Bias Corrections
- **Authors/org:** Joseph Korkames; T. D. Stanley; Stefan Stremersch
- **Year:** 2025/2026
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis / bias correction
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF from author university page
- **Link:** https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf
- **Why read:** Important antidote to overconfident marketing claims; focuses on how bias inflates measured ad effects.
- **Bias note:** Academic; explicitly focused on correcting publication/misspecification/aggregation bias
### A Meta-Analysis of When and How Advertising Creativity Works
- **Authors/org:** Sara Rosengren; Martin Eisend; Scott Koslow; Micael Dahlen
- **Year:** 2020
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher page and open author PDFs available
- **Link:** https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0022242920929288
- **Why read:** Teaches when creative advertising changes responses and when it does not.
- **Bias note:** Academic; creativity effects depend heavily on measurement and context
### The Long and the Short of It: Balancing Short and Long-Term Marketing Strategies
- **Authors/org:** Les Binet; Peter Field
- **Year:** 2013
- **Type:** Practitioner/industry effectiveness analysis
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** IPA report; versions/slides publicly available
- **Link:** https://ipa.co.uk/knowledge/publications-reports/the-long-and-the-short-of-it-balancing-short-and-long-term-marketing-strategies
- **Why read:** Separates short-term activation from long-term brand building; useful for avoiding click-obsession.
- **Bias note:** Practitioner and award-case database; useful but not neutral academic evidence
### Does Advertising Work? Evidence from the Past and Some New Evidence
- **Authors/org:** Russ Winer
- **Year:** 2012
- **Type:** Academic presentation / review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.gc.cuny.edu/sites/default/files/2021-07/Does-Avertising-Work--Winer.pdf
- **Why read:** Readable overview of advertising elasticities and historical evidence.
- **Bias note:** Lecture deck; interpret as synthesis, not primary evidence

## Attention and ad avoidance
### Banner Blindness: Web Searchers Often Miss “Obvious” Links
- **Authors/org:** Jan Panero Benway; David M. Lane
- **Year:** 1998/2007
- **Type:** Usability research paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.ruf.rice.edu/~lane/papers/banner_blindness.pdf
- **Why read:** Primary evidence for why ad-like elements get ignored.
- **Bias note:** Old web context but human attention pattern remains useful
### Banner Blindness Revisited: Users Dodge Ads on Mobile and Desktop
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen Norman Group
- **Year:** 2018
- **Type:** UX research article
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-old-and-new-findings/
- **Why read:** Modern applied summary of banner blindness and ad-like UI avoidance.
- **Bias note:** UX consultancy; credible but commercial
### Banner Blindness: The Original Eyetracking Research
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen Norman Group
- **Year:** 2007
- **Type:** UX research article
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-original-eyetracking/
- **Why read:** Useful visual attention framing for ad/landing page design.
- **Bias note:** UX consultancy; interpret alongside academic work

## B2B software buying
### 2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report
- **Authors/org:** G2
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor/industry report
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf
- **Why read:** Current B2B software buyer behavior, shortlist dynamics, AI/review influence.

- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor/industry report
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-Bridging-the-Trust-Gap-B2B-Tech-Buying-in-the-Age-of-AI.pdf
- **Why read:** Highlights transparent pricing, AI-assisted research, fact-checking, and trust gaps.
- **Bias note:** Review marketplace with commercial incentive; still useful buyer survey data
### Gartner Sales Survey Finds 61% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Buying Experience
- **Authors/org:** Gartner
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Analyst research press release
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page; full research may require subscription
- **Link:** https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2025-06-25-gartner-sales-survey-finds-61-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-buying-experience
- **Why read:** Important for self-serve proof architecture and low-pressure buying paths.

### Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience
- **Authors/org:** Gartner
- **Year:** 2026
- **Type:** Analyst research press release
- **Priority:** Useful/current
- **Access:** Open web page; full research may require subscription
- **Link:** https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience
- **Why read:** Current signal that self-directed buying continues strengthening.
- **Bias note:** Analyst firm; current but press-release level
### How B2B SaaS Marketing Leaders Buy Software in 2024
- **Authors/org:** Wynter
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Vendor research report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://info.wynter.com/hubfs/B2B%20buying%20journey%20study%20by%20Wynter.pdf
- **Why read:** Good for messaging, shortlists, peer/review/site influence in SaaS.

### 2025 Buyer Behavior Report
- **Authors/org:** Consensus
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor/industry report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web/report
- **Link:** https://goconsensus.com/research/2025-b2b-buyer-behavior-report
- **Why read:** Good current view of how product experiences support buyer self-education.
- **Bias note:** Interactive demo vendor; may overstate demo/product-tour value

## Brand strategy
### How Brands Grow
- **Authors/org:** Byron Sharp
- **Year:** 2010
- **Type:** Book / evidence-based marketing
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Buy/borrow; summaries online
- **Link:** https://marketingscience.info/news-and-insights/grow-brand-grow
- **Why read:** Mental availability, physical availability, distinctive assets; useful counterweight to narrow performance marketing.
- **Bias note:** Ehrenberg-Bass school has strong viewpoint; compare with segmentation/positioning literature
### The DNA of Distinctive Brands: Guide to Distinctive Brand Assets
- **Authors/org:** Distinctive BAT
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Practitioner guide
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.distinctivebat.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/The-DNA-of-Distinctive-Brands-Distinctive-BAT-Guide-To-DBAs.pdf
- **Why read:** Practical way to apply distinctive brand asset thinking to digital products.
- **Bias note:** Commercial consultancy; useful framework but verify with independent evidence

## Buyer psychology
### A Behavior Model for Persuasive Design
- **Authors/org:** B. J. Fogg
- **Year:** 2009
- **Type:** Academic conference paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.demenzemedicinagenerale.net/images/mens-sana/Captology_Fogg_Behavior_Model.pdf
- **Why read:** Motivation + ability + prompt is the simplest usable model for conversion behavior.
- **Bias note:** Persuasive design lens; can be misused if not ethically constrained
### The Fogg Behavior Model
- **Authors/org:** Stanford Behavior Design Lab
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** Official framework page
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web page
- **Link:** https://behaviordesign.stanford.edu/resources/fogg-behavior-model
- **Why read:** Good quick reference for the behavior model.
- **Bias note:** Official model page; simplified
### The Elaboration Likelihood Model of Persuasion
- **Authors/org:** Richard E. Petty; John T. Cacioppo
- **Year:** 1986
- **Type:** Foundational persuasion theory chapter
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://richardepetty.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/1986-advances-pettycacioppo.pdf
- **Why read:** Separates deep argument processing from surface-cue persuasion.
- **Bias note:** Classic model; not a conversion playbook by itself

## Customer journey
### The Consumer Decision Journey
- **Authors/org:** David Court; Dave Elzinga; Susan Mulder; Ole Jørgen Vetvik
- **Year:** 2009
- **Type:** Consulting/practitioner model
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-consumer-decision-journey
- **Why read:** Useful replacement for simplistic linear funnels.
- **Bias note:** Consulting framework; not peer-reviewed
### The New Consumer Decision Journey
- **Authors/org:** McKinsey
- **Year:** 2015
- **Type:** Consulting/practitioner model
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-new-consumer-decision-journey
- **Why read:** Adds digital feedback loops and active evaluation.
- **Bias note:** Consulting perspective

## Customer research
### Know Your Customers' Jobs to Be Done
- **Authors/org:** Clayton M. Christensen; Taddy Hall; Karen Dillon; David S. Duncan
- **Year:** 2016
- **Type:** HBR article / strategy framework
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** HBR; PDF copies may exist
- **Link:** https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done
- **Why read:** Teaches that customers “hire” products to make progress in a situation.
- **Bias note:** Practitioner/consulting style; use with interviews, not slogans

## Digital advertising measurement
### Online Advertising
- **Authors/org:** Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker
- **Year:** 2011/2014
- **Type:** Academic handbook chapter
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://www-2.rotman.utoronto.ca/~agoldfarb/OnlineAdvertising.pdf
- **Why read:** Explains targeting, measurement, privacy, and economic differences between online and offline advertising.
- **Bias note:** Academic overview; older but still foundational
### Privacy Regulation and Online Advertising
- **Authors/org:** Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker
- **Year:** 2011
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed research paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Privacy-Regulation-and-Online-Advertising.pdf
- **Why read:** Shows how privacy rules and targeting interact with ad effectiveness.
- **Bias note:** Academic; context predates today’s privacy stack but mechanisms matter
### Measuring the Effects of Advertising: The Digital Frontier
- **Authors/org:** Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao; David H. Reiley
- **Year:** 2013/2015
- **Type:** NBER/academic chapter
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open NBER PDF
- **Link:** https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w19520/w19520.pdf
- **Why read:** Clicks are easy to count; causal ad lift is hard. This paper teaches that distinction.
- **Bias note:** Authors have worked at ad platforms; still one of the strongest measurement papers
### The Unfavorable Economics of Measuring the Returns to Advertising
- **Authors/org:** Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao
- **Year:** 2015
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed field experiment evidence
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher/SSRN; open copies available
- **Link:** https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2367103
- **Why read:** Shows why measured ROI confidence intervals can be huge; prevents dashboard worship.
- **Bias note:** Strong empirical design; not a “marketing is useless” paper, a measurement precision paper
### A Comparison of Approaches to Advertising Measurement: Evidence from Big Field Experiments at Facebook
- **Authors/org:** Brett R. Gordon; Florian Zettelmeyer; Neha Bhargava; Daniel Chapsky
- **Year:** 2018/2019
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed field experiment comparison
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf
- **Why read:** Contrasts randomized experiments with observational measurement and shows why endogeneity matters.
- **Bias note:** Facebook experiment data; platform context matters, but methodology lessons are broad
### The Online Display Ad Effectiveness Funnel & Carryover: Lessons from 432 Field Experiments
- **Authors/org:** Garrett A. Johnson; Randall A. Lewis; Elmar I. Nubbemeyer
- **Year:** 2017
- **Type:** Field experiment meta-study
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Johnson-Garrett-PAPER-VERSION-2.pdf
- **Why read:** Useful for understanding small ad effects, funnel stages, and carryover.
- **Bias note:** Display-ad platform setting; still valuable scale and causal design
### Consumer Heterogeneity and Paid Search Effectiveness: A Large-Scale Field Experiment
- **Authors/org:** Thomas Blake; Chris Nosko; Steven Tadelis
- **Year:** 2015
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed field experiment
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://faculty.haas.berkeley.edu/stadelis/BNT_ECMA_rev.pdf
- **Why read:** Shows paid search can be weak for already-known brands but useful for new/infrequent users.
- **Bias note:** eBay context; generalize carefully
### Online Ads and Offline Sales: Measuring the Effects of Retail Advertising via a Controlled Experiment on Yahoo!
- **Authors/org:** Randall A. Lewis; David H. Reiley
- **Year:** 2014
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed controlled experiment
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://www.davidreiley.com/papers/OnlineAdsOfflineSales.pdf
- **Why read:** Important reminder that many ad effects may happen without clicks.
- **Bias note:** Retail context; still useful for click vs non-click impact
### Online Display Advertising Markets: A Literature Review and Future Directions
- **Authors/org:** Hana Choi; Carl Mela; Santiago R. Balseiro; Adam Leary
- **Year:** 2019
- **Type:** Academic literature review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://hanachoi.github.io/research-papers/choi_et_al_review_display_advertising.pdf
- **Why read:** Map of the display advertising ecosystem, auctions, targeting, advertisers, publishers, and consumers.
- **Bias note:** Academic review; technical

## Ethics and dark patterns
### Sludge Audits
- **Authors/org:** Cass R. Sunstein
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed public policy paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher/SSRN
- **Link:** https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/behavioural-public-policy/article/sludge-audits/12A7E338984CE8807CC1E078EC4F13A7
- **Why read:** Convert ethically by removing unjustified friction; do not add sludge to trap people.
- **Bias note:** Policy perspective; useful ethical lens
### Fixing Frictions: Sludge Audits Around the World
- **Authors/org:** OECD
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Government/intergovernmental report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2024/06/fixing-frictions-sludge-audits-around-the-world_1b4bbf1a/5e9bb35c-en.pdf
- **Why read:** Good practical approach to auditing harmful friction.
- **Bias note:** Public-sector lens; not commercial conversion-focused
### Bringing Dark Patterns to Light
- **Authors/org:** U.S. Federal Trade Commission
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Government/regulator report
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/ftc_gov/pdf/P214800%2BDark%2BPatterns%2BReport%2B9.14.2022%2B-%2BFINAL.pdf
- **Why read:** Clear boundaries between persuasion and deception/manipulation.
- **Bias note:** Regulator lens; emphasizes consumer harm
### Dark Commercial Patterns
- **Authors/org:** OECD
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Intergovernmental evidence report
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/dark-commercial-patterns_44f5e846-en.html
- **Why read:** Defines dark patterns, prevalence, harms, and responses.
- **Bias note:** Policy/regulator lens; not a growth handbook
### Online Choice Architecture: How Digital Design Can Harm Competition and Consumers
- **Authors/org:** UK Competition and Markets Authority
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Regulator discussion paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF/web
- **Link:** https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/online-choice-architecture-how-digital-design-can-harm-competition-and-consumers
- **Why read:** Taxonomy of online choice architecture practices: defaults, scarcity, friction, prompts, ranking, etc.
- **Bias note:** Competition/consumer protection lens
### Evidence Review of Online Choice Architecture and Consumer and Competition Harm
- **Authors/org:** UK CMA
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Regulator evidence review
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open 261-page PDF
- **Link:** https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6258499fd3bf7f600e76d9de/OCA_Evidence_Review_Paper_14.4.22.pdf
- **Why read:** Large document with academic evidence behind online choice architecture.
- **Bias note:** Regulator/evidence review; deep but harm-oriented
### Dark Patterns at Scale: Findings from a Crawl of 11K Shopping Websites
- **Authors/org:** Arunesh Mathur et al.
- **Year:** 2019
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed HCI paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open arXiv/PDF
- **Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.07032
- **Why read:** Empirical taxonomy of dark patterns at scale.
- **Bias note:** Shopping-site focus; taxonomy useful across digital products
### ICPEN Dark Patterns in Subscription Services Sweep Public Report
- **Authors/org:** ICPEN consumer protection agencies
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** International regulator sweep report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.oaic.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0018/232623/Public-Report-ICPEN-Dark-Patterns-Sweep.pdf
- **Why read:** Very relevant to SaaS subscriptions, cancellation, and recurring billing.
- **Bias note:** Regulator lens; subscription focus
### Duped by Design
- **Authors/org:** Consumer Policy Research Centre
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Consumer policy report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://cprc.org.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/CPRC-Duped-by-Design-Final-Report-June-2022.pdf
- **Why read:** Good regional dark-pattern context and examples.
- **Bias note:** Consumer advocacy lens; Australia-relevant
### Review of Online Choice Architecture and Vulnerability
- **Authors/org:** Behavioural Insights Team
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Research review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.bi.team/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Review-of-Online-Choice-Architecture-and-Vulnerability-July-2024-.pdf
- **Why read:** Important for understanding vulnerable users and ethical design.
- **Bias note:** Policy/behavioral science perspective

## Experimentation
### Online Controlled Experiments at Large Scale
- **Authors/org:** Ron Kohavi et al.
- **Year:** 2013
- **Type:** Academic/practitioner paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://exp-platform.com/Documents/2013%20controlledExperimentsAtScale.pdf
- **Why read:** A/B testing fundamentals, scale issues, organizational discipline.
- **Bias note:** Written by experimentation practitioners; highly practical
### Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments: A Practical Guide to A/B Testing
- **Authors/org:** Ron Kohavi; Diane Tang; Ya Xu
- **Year:** 2020
- **Type:** Book
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Buy/borrow; publisher previews available
- **Link:** https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/trustworthy-online-controlled-experiments/4EC18E75D4F1D3A576B28E0CBD49F5B9
- **Why read:** Best practical guide to running experiments without fooling yourself.
- **Bias note:** Practitioner-led but rigorous; book is not free in full
### Unexpected Results in Online Controlled Experiments
- **Authors/org:** Ron Kohavi; Roger Longbotham
- **Year:** 2010
- **Type:** Academic/practitioner paper
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://kdd.org/exploration_files/v12-02-8-UR-Kohavi.pdf
- **Why read:** Teaches humility: experiments often produce counterintuitive results.
- **Bias note:** Practitioner perspective; valuable failure modes

### Digital 2025: Global Overview Report

- **Access:** Open web report

### Media Reactions 2025

### Trust in Advertising Study
