## Pricing and monetization
### Consumer Reactions to Drip Pricing
- **Authors/org:** S. Santana; S. Dallas; V. G. Morwitz
- **Year:** 2020
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **Link:** https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Morwitz-Vicki-Wroe-Alderson-PAPER-2020.pdf
- **Why read:** Shows how price presentation changes behavior and perceived fairness.
- **Bias note:** Specific pricing mechanism; important ethical boundary
### Consumers' Reactions to Partitioned Prices
- **Authors/org:** Vicki G. Morwitz; Eric A. Greenleaf; Eric J. Johnson
- **Year:** 1998
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Publisher/JSTOR
- **Link:** https://www.jstor.org/stable/pdf/3152164.pdf
- **Why read:** Explains why separating price components can change recall and demand.
- **Bias note:** Classic paper; older and often misused by dark-pattern marketers
### A Review of Research on Partitioned Pricing
- **Authors/org:** Eric A. Greenleaf et al.
- **Year:** 2016
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed literature review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Publisher page
- **Link:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S105774081500042X
- **Why read:** Context for price framing and price transparency.
- **Bias note:** Review literature; not SaaS-specific
### Behavioral Response to Price: Data-Based Insights and Future Research Directions
- **Authors/org:** Gurumurthy Kalyanaram et al.
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Publisher page
- **Link:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0022435922000124
- **Why read:** Good map of price perception, reference price, and response.
- **Bias note:** Retail-oriented pricing literature

## SaaS/product-led growth
### Converting Free Users to Paid Subscribers in the SaaS Context
- **Authors/org:** Hongshuang (Alice) Li
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed SaaS marketing paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher/SSRN; open PDF available
- **Link:** https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3945909
- **Why read:** Directly connects marketing touchpoints, message content, usage, and conversion.
- **Bias note:** Single SaaS-firm data; excellent relevance, limited generality
### Longer or Shorter? A Large-Scale Randomized Field Experiment on Free Trial Duration in the Freemium Model
- **Authors/org:** Ling Zhang; Jiang Duan
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Open-access field experiment
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PMC/Frontiers article
- **Link:** https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12217587/
- **Why read:** Trial length affects adoption and delayed conversion differently from immediate conversion.
- **Bias note:** One SaaS company; still valuable because randomized and large-scale
### Freemium Pricing: Evidence from a Large-Scale Field Experiment
- **Authors/org:** Julian Runge et al.
- **Year:** 2016
- **Type:** Working paper / field experiment
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/155324/1/880313099.pdf
- **Why read:** Helps think beyond “free = growth” toward long-term profit and conversion.
- **Bias note:** Game/software context; use as freemium mechanics evidence
### The Zero-Price Effect in Freemium Business Models
- **Authors/org:** Thomas Niemand et al.
- **Year:** 2019
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher page; abstracts/open copies
- **Link:** https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/mar.21211
- **Why read:** Explains why free tiers can both reduce friction and anchor users against paying.
- **Bias note:** Behavioral experiments; not a complete GTM model
### Designing Freemium with Usage Limitation
- **Authors/org:** Takumi Kato
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed modeling paper
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Publisher abstract
- **Link:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1567422322000370
- **Why read:** Useful for deciding whether to limit features, seats, usage, storage, or outputs.
- **Bias note:** Model-based; validate against real product data
### The SaaS Conversion Report
- **Authors/org:** ChartMogul
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** Vendor/industry benchmark
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web report
- **Link:** https://chartmogul.com/reports/saas-conversion-report-2/
- **Why read:** Benchmarks for trial/freemium conversion; useful but not law.
- **Bias note:** Subscription analytics vendor; benchmarks are directional
### Product Benchmarks / Product-Led Growth Benchmarks
- **Authors/org:** OpenView
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** VC/practitioner benchmark
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web reports
- **Link:** https://openviewpartners.com/benchmarks/
- **Why read:** Good for SaaS benchmarks, PLG vocabulary, and metrics literacy.
- **Bias note:** VC lens; data set selection and incentives matter

## Skepticism and trust
### The Persuasion Knowledge Model: How People Cope with Persuasion Attempts
- **Authors/org:** Marian Friestad; Peter Wright
- **Year:** 1994
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed theory paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher page; library may be needed
- **Link:** https://academic.oup.com/jcr/article/21/1/1/1853712
- **Why read:** Explains the mental guard that activates when people notice “someone is trying to persuade me.”
- **Bias note:** Theory paper; requires application judgment
### Ad Skepticism: The Consequences of Disbelief
- **Authors/org:** Carl Obermiller; Eric Spangenberg; Douglas L. MacLachlan
- **Year:** 2005
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher page/open copies
- **Link:** https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00913367.2005.10639199
- **Why read:** Directly addresses your instinct to disbelieve ad claims.
- **Bias note:** Academic; specific measures of skepticism
### Beyond Martech: Building Trust with Consumers and Engaging Where Sentiment Is High
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen
- **Year:** 2021
- **Type:** Industry research / trust in advertising
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.nielsen.com/insights/2021/beyond-martech-building-trust-with-consumers-and-engaging-where-sentiment-is-high/
- **Why read:** Shows relative trust in word-of-mouth vs paid ad channels.
- **Bias note:** Measurement company; still useful for channel-trust comparisons
### The Effect of Characteristics of Source Credibility on Consumer Behaviour: A Meta-Analysis
- **Authors/org:** Elvira Ismagilova et al.
- **Year:** 2020
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher page; abstracts/open versions
- **Link:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698918307926
- **Why read:** Explains why expertise, trustworthiness, and similarity affect adoption of messages.
- **Bias note:** Academic meta-analysis; mostly eWOM contexts
### How Online Reviews Affect Purchase Intention: A Meta-Analysis Across Contextual and Cultural Factors
- **Authors/org:** Keda Qiu; Liyi Zhang
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open ScienceDirect article
- **Link:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2543925123000323
- **Why read:** Reviews are a major trust layer after the ad earns inspection.
- **Bias note:** Review literature heterogeneity; still strong source
### Unraveling the Personalization Paradox
- **Authors/org:** Elizabeth Aguirre; Dominik Mahr; Dhruv Grewal; Ko de Ruyter; Martin Wetzels
- **Year:** 2015
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open institutional PDF
- **Link:** https://cris.maastrichtuniversity.nl/ws/files/65443620/mahr_2015_unraveling_the_personalization_paradox.pdf
- **Why read:** Shows personalization can help or backfire depending on perceived data collection.
- **Bias note:** Experimental settings; implications depend on current privacy norms
### Meta-Analytic Review of Online Purchase Intention
- **Authors/org:** Moumita Ghosh et al.
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed meta-analysis
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/325948/1/10.1080_23311975.2023.2296686.pdf
- **Why read:** Good broad map of online purchase-intention drivers.
- **Bias note:** Broad e-commerce scope; not SaaS-specific

## Software adoption
### Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Ease of Use, and User Acceptance of Information Technology
- **Authors/org:** Fred D. Davis
- **Year:** 1989
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Publisher/JSTOR; summaries available
- **Link:** https://www.jstor.org/stable/249008
- **Why read:** Core SaaS adoption equation: useful + easy to use.
- **Bias note:** Classic model; later models add context, norms, and habit
### Technology Acceptance Model Overview
- **Authors/org:** TheoryHub / Newcastle University
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Theory overview
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://open.ncl.ac.uk/theory-library/technology-acceptance-model.pdf
- **Why read:** Readable overview of TAM before/after the original Davis paper.
- **Bias note:** Secondary summary
### Detailed Review of Rogers' Diffusion of Innovations Theory
- **Authors/org:** Ismail Sahin
- **Year:** 2006
- **Type:** Academic review paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **Link:** https://tilisathibodeaux.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Sahin.pdf
- **Why read:** Adoption categories, perceived attributes of innovation, uncertainty reduction.
- **Bias note:** Secondary review of Rogers; education-tech angle

## UX and credibility
### Trustworthiness in Web Design: 4 Credibility Factors
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen Norman Group
- **Year:** 2016
- **Type:** UX research article
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.nngroup.com/articles/trustworthy-design/
- **Why read:** Useful design checklist for landing pages and SaaS websites.
- **Bias note:** UX consultancy; practical rather than academic
### Ecommerce Usability Improvements
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen Norman Group
- **Year:** 2011
- **Type:** UX research article
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Open web article
- **Link:** https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ecommerce-improvements/
- **Why read:** Useful reminder that content and usability failures block purchases.
- **Bias note:** E-commerce focus; translate carefully to SaaS
### Ecommerce User Experience: Trust and Credibility
- **Authors/org:** Nielsen Norman Group
- **Year:** Current edition
- **Type:** Paid UX report
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Paid report page
- **Link:** https://www.nngroup.com/reports/ecommerce-ux-trust-and-credibility/
- **Why read:** Detailed trust/credibility recommendations if you want deep UX reading.
- **Bias note:** Paid consultancy report; valuable if accessible
### Checkout UX / Ecommerce UX Research
- **Authors/org:** Baymard Institute
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** UX benchmark/research
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Some free articles; full reports paid
- **Link:** https://baymard.com/research
- **Why read:** Very practical for checkout friction, forms, pricing pages, and trust.
- **Bias note:** UX research company; credible but commercial


# Generative AI, AI Search, Agentic Commerce, and Governance
These sources were added in the deeper AI edition. Treat academic, regulator, and security sources as the backbone. Treat platform/vendor/consultancy reports as current market signals, not neutral truth.

## How generative AI Is shaping the future of marketing

- **Authors/org:** Dhruv Grewal; Cinthia B. Satornino; Thomas Davenport; Abhijit Guha
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed conceptual/review article
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Springer page; open/full text may vary
- **URL:** https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01064-3
- **Bias / caveat:** Academic; broad strategy lens; not a tactical implementation manual
- **Why study it:** High-level map of how GenAI changes marketing research, strategy, actions, customer experience, and firm processes.

## Generative AI in innovation and marketing processes

- **Authors/org:** Paola Cillo; Gaia Rubera
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed roadmap/research opportunities
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Springer page
- **URL:** https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01044-7
- **Bias / caveat:** Academic; opportunity framing
- **Why study it:** Useful for seeing GenAI as part of product, innovation, and marketing processes rather than copy generation alone.

## Transforming advertising in the age of generative AI

- **Authors/org:** J. Yang
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed exploratory study
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Taylor & Francis page
- **URL:** https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2025.2604910
- **Bias / caveat:** Qualitative/exploratory; not causal performance evidence
- **Why study it:** Shows how advertising practitioners integrate GenAI into creative and production workflows.

## Frontiers: Supporting Content Marketing with Natural Language Generation

- **Authors/org:** Martin Reisenbichler; Thomas Reutterer; David A. Schweidel; Daniel Dan
- **Year:** 2022
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed Marketing Science article
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open author PDF
- **URL:** https://www.reutterer.com/papers/reisenbichler%26reutterer%26schweidel%26dan_2022.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Academic; pre-ChatGPT NLG context but still methodologically useful
- **Why study it:** Grounds AI content use in semi-automated content marketing and SEO rather than vague productivity claims.

## Using LLMs for Market Research

- **Authors/org:** James Brand; Ayelet Israeli; Donald Ngwe
- **Year:** 2023
- **Type:** Harvard Business School working paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://www.hbs.edu/ris/Publication%20Files/23-062_6bfe7a5b-3ed9-4afd-a4c1-c91b60dd82e5.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Academic working paper; methods still developing
- **Why study it:** Shows how LLMs may be used to simulate or augment market research, while requiring careful validation against real human data.

## Large Language Models for Market Research: A Data Augmentation Approach

- **Authors/org:** M. Wang et al.
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** arXiv research paper
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open arXiv
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19363
- **Bias / caveat:** Preprint; not final peer-reviewed; technical-statistical focus
- **Why study it:** Important warning: LLM-generated responses can help, but must be debiased/anchored with real human data.

## GEO: Generative Engine Optimization

- **Authors/org:** P. Aggarwal et al.
- **Year:** 2023/2024
- **Type:** arXiv / research paper
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.09735
- **Bias / caveat:** Early research; engines and products evolve quickly
- **Why study it:** Introduces the idea that visibility in generative answer engines requires different tactics from classic SEO.

## Generative Engine Optimization: How to Dominate AI Search

- **Authors/org:** Mahe Chen; Xiaoxuan Wang; Kaiwen Chen; Nick Koudas
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** arXiv empirical analysis
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open arXiv
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.08919
- **Bias / caveat:** Preprint; commercial relevance high but findings may shift as engines update
- **Why study it:** Compares AI search and traditional search; emphasizes earned media, source authority, freshness, and machine-readable justification.

## Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile

- **Authors/org:** NIST
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Government risk management framework
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Government/standards perspective; not marketing-specific
- **Why study it:** Core risk-management source for GenAI use: validity, privacy, security, transparency, and governance.

## NIST AI Risk Management Framework

- **Authors/org:** NIST
- **Year:** 2023/2024
- **Type:** Government standards framework
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open web page and PDFs
- **URL:** https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework
- **Bias / caveat:** Government/standards perspective
- **Why study it:** Use as your baseline for trustworthy AI management across design, deployment, monitoring, and governance.

## OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

- **Authors/org:** OWASP GenAI Security Project
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Security guidance
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open web page and PDF
- **URL:** https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/
- **Bias / caveat:** Security-focused; conservative by design
- **Why study it:** Needed if building AI chatbots, sales agents, support agents, website agents, or internal marketing automations.

## The 2025 AI Index Report

- **Authors/org:** Stanford HAI
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Annual AI index report
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open web page/PDF
- **URL:** https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report
- **Bias / caveat:** Academic/institutional; broad AI scope, not marketing-specific
- **Why study it:** Current macro baseline for AI adoption, investment, capabilities, policy, and public perception.

## The State of AI: Global Survey 2025

- **Authors/org:** McKinsey & Company / QuantumBlack
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Consultancy survey/report
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open article/PDF
- **URL:** https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai
- **Bias / caveat:** Consultancy; useful signal but incentive to frame AI transformation opportunities
- **Why study it:** Current business adoption signal; useful for seeing which functions use AI and how firms report value.

## The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025

- **Authors/org:** MIT Project NANDA
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Research report
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF mirror
- **URL:** https://mlq.ai/media/quarterly_decks/v0.1_State_of_AI_in_Business_2025_Report.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Preliminary findings; methodology should be read carefully
- **Why study it:** Counterweight to hype: argues many GenAI projects fail because they are not integrated into workflows.

## FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes

- **Authors/org:** Federal Trade Commission
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Regulator enforcement release
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes
- **Bias / caveat:** Regulator perspective; enforcement-focused
- **Why study it:** Essential warning against overclaiming AI capabilities, earnings outcomes, fake reviews, and AI-washing.

## FTC Artificial Intelligence Industry Page

- **Authors/org:** Federal Trade Commission
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** Regulator hub
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence
- **Bias / caveat:** Regulator perspective
- **Why study it:** Use to monitor AI enforcement trends around deception, data use, endorsements, and consumer harm.

## Truth in Advertising

- **Authors/org:** Federal Trade Commission
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** Regulator guidance hub
- **Priority:** Essential
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/topics/truth-advertising
- **Bias / caveat:** Regulator perspective
- **Why study it:** Base rule: advertising claims must be truthful, not misleading, and substantiated where appropriate.

## OECD AI Principles

- **Authors/org:** OECD
- **Year:** 2019/2024 update
- **Type:** International policy principles
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://oecd.ai/en/ai-principles
- **Bias / caveat:** Policy lens; high-level
- **Why study it:** Good global baseline for trustworthy AI: human rights, transparency, robustness, accountability.

## EU AI Act regulatory framework

- **Authors/org:** European Commission
- **Year:** 2024-2026
- **Type:** Regulatory framework page
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai
- **Bias / caveat:** EU regulatory perspective
- **Why study it:** Required if marketing or AI products reach EU users; useful baseline for risk-based AI governance.

## General-Purpose AI Code of Practice

- **Authors/org:** European Commission
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Regulatory code of practice
- **Priority:** Useful/current
- **Access:** Open web page and downloadable chapters
- **URL:** https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/contents-code-gpai
- **Bias / caveat:** EU regulatory perspective; provider-focused
- **Why study it:** Useful for understanding transparency, copyright, and safety expectations around general-purpose AI models.

## About ads and AI Overviews

- **Authors/org:** Google Ads Help
- **Year:** Current
- **Type:** Platform documentation
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16297775?hl=en
- **Bias / caveat:** Platform-owned; describes Google’s product framing
- **Why study it:** Source for how ads are integrated into AI Overviews and how AI search changes ad surfaces.

## Google Marketing Live 2025: News and Updates

- **Authors/org:** Google
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Platform announcement
- **Priority:** Useful/current
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-marketing-live-2025/
- **Bias / caveat:** Platform marketing announcement; product-promotion bias
- **Why study it:** Tracks current AI ad products, AI Mode/AI Overviews advertising, and campaign automation direction.

## Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol

- **Authors/org:** OpenAI
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Platform announcement
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://openai.com/index/buy-it-in-chatgpt/
- **Bias / caveat:** Platform-owned; product-promotion bias
- **Why study it:** Signals the movement from AI-assisted discovery to AI-mediated checkout and agentic commerce.

## Powering Product Discovery in ChatGPT

- **Authors/org:** OpenAI
- **Year:** 2026
- **Type:** Platform announcement
- **Priority:** High/current
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/
- **Bias / caveat:** Platform-owned; product-promotion bias
- **Why study it:** Current signal for AI-native product discovery, comparison, and shopping behavior.

## The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants

- **Authors/org:** McKinsey & Company
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Consultancy report
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-agentic-commerce-opportunity-how-ai-agents-are-ushering-in-a-new-era-for-consumers-and-merchants
- **Bias / caveat:** Consultancy; opportunity framing
- **Why study it:** Useful for thinking beyond ads: buyer agents, merchant data, checkout, product feeds, and trust.

## 2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report

- **Authors/org:** G2
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor/review-platform survey report
- **Priority:** High/current
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Review platform; commercially biased toward review/research channels
- **Why study it:** Useful current signal that AI search and review sites increasingly shape B2B software evaluation.

## B2B Tech Buying in the Age of AI

- **Authors/org:** TrustRadius
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor/review-platform buyer report
- **Priority:** High/current
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-2025-Buyer-Research-Report-Top-10-Takeaways.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Review platform; commercially biased, but useful buyer-behavior signal
- **Why study it:** Highlights AI Overviews, fact-checking, prior experience, pricing transparency, and the trust gap in B2B tech buying.

## Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience

- **Authors/org:** Gartner
- **Year:** 2026
- **Type:** Analyst press release
- **Priority:** High/current
- **Access:** Open web page
- **URL:** https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience
- **Bias / caveat:** Analyst firm; press release is summarized, not full methodology
- **Why study it:** Reinforces self-serve/software-buyer trend and the need to build AI-assisted buyer support instead of relying only on reps.

## Generative AI Playbook for Advertising

- **Authors/org:** IAB
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Industry playbook
- **Priority:** Useful/current
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://www.iab.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/IAB_GenerativeAIPlaybook_January_26.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Industry association; practice-heavy, less neutral than academic sources
- **Why study it:** Operational view of GenAI use cases, campaign optimization, production, creative, and governance.

## Generative AI enhances individual creativity but reduces the collective diversity of novel content

- **Authors/org:** Anil R. Doshi; Oliver P. Hauser
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed Science Advances article
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Publisher page
- **URL:** https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adn5290
- **Bias / caveat:** Specific creative-writing context; generalize carefully
- **Why study it:** Important caution: AI can improve average output but flatten diversity. This matters for ads and brand differentiation.

## The AI-authorship effect: Understanding authenticity, moral disgust, and consumer responses to AI-generated marketing communications

- **Authors/org:** C. P. Kirk; J. Givi
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed Journal of Business Research article
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Abstract/index page
- **URL:** https://ideas.repec.org/a/eee/jbrese/v186y2025ics0148296324004880.html
- **Bias / caveat:** Experimental context; emotional communications may not generalize to all software ads
- **Why study it:** Shows that AI authorship can reduce authenticity, positive word of mouth, and loyalty for some emotional communications.

## The transparency dilemma: How AI disclosure erodes trust

- **Authors/org:** Oliver Schilke et al.
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed article
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** ScienceDirect page
- **URL:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0749597825000172
- **Bias / caveat:** Context dependent; transparency can be legally/ethically necessary even if trust penalty exists
- **Why study it:** Nuanced source: disclosure can reduce trust, so transparency strategy must be handled carefully, not mechanically.

## Effect of disclosing AI-generated content on prosocial advertising

- **Authors/org:** T. H. Baek et al.
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed advertising study
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Taylor & Francis page
- **URL:** https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2024.2401319
- **Bias / caveat:** Prosocial advertising context; not directly SaaS
- **Why study it:** Good example of how disclosure effects are context-sensitive and can influence attitudes/intentions.

## Appeal types, self-efficacy and AI's social role

- **Authors/org:** Y. Chen et al.
- **Year:** 2024
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed experimental article
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** ScienceDirect page
- **URL:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0148296324003710
- **Bias / caveat:** Experimental; limited contexts
- **Why study it:** Useful for choosing whether AI-created ads should use practical/agentic vs warm/communal appeals.

## Generative AI advertisements and Human–AI collaboration

- **Authors/org:** J. C. Madathil et al.
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Peer-reviewed advertising study
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** ScienceDirect page
- **URL:** https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698925001602
- **Bias / caveat:** Specific to video ads and human-AI cues
- **Why study it:** Useful for thinking about when human involvement should remain visible to protect perceived humanity and reduce anxiety.

## How AI-generated ads' visual cues shape consumer behavior

- **Authors/org:** Y. Exner et al.
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Research paper / quasi-experimental advertising data
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open PDF
- **URL:** https://www.columbia.edu/~on2110/Papers/AI_in_disguise.pdf
- **Bias / caveat:** Specific dataset and ad context; quasi-experimental assumptions matter
- **Why study it:** Useful because it links AI-generated visual cues to real ad behavior rather than opinions alone.

## Collaborating with AI Agents: A Field Experiment on Ad Creation

- **Authors/org:** Research authors via arXiv
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** arXiv/field experiment
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open arXiv HTML
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2503.18238v2
- **Bias / caveat:** Preprint; evaluate methods carefully
- **Why study it:** Useful field evidence on human-AI collaboration in ad creation, productivity, and output quality.

## Generative AI and Firm Productivity: Field Experiments in Online Retail

- **Authors/org:** L. Fang et al.
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** arXiv/field experiment
- **Priority:** High
- **Access:** Open arXiv HTML / PDF
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/html/2510.12049v1
- **Bias / caveat:** Preprint; online-retail setting; productivity != brand trust
- **Why study it:** Shows GenAI value emerges from embedded workflows, not isolated prompts.

## 2025 AI and Digital Trends in B2B Journeys

- **Authors/org:** Adobe
- **Year:** 2025
- **Type:** Vendor report
- **Priority:** Useful/current
- **Access:** Report page/gated in places
- **URL:** https://business.adobe.com/resources/reports/b2b-marketing-digital-trends.html
- **Bias / caveat:** Vendor; promotes enterprise marketing software worldview
- **Why study it:** Useful market signal for AI-driven B2B journeys, content effectiveness, and personalization.

## Consumer Acceptance of Conversational Bots: Systematic Literature Review

- **Authors/org:** O. H. Fares et al.
- **Year:** 2025/2026
- **Type:** Systematic literature review
- **Priority:** Useful
- **Access:** Publisher page
- **URL:** https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/cb.70130
- **Bias / caveat:** Review article; customer-service/chatbot context varies
- **Why study it:** Useful for designing AI chat interfaces that buyers can trust and use rather than resent.

# Source Index

Converted from `03_source_index.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| key | category | priority | title | authors | year | type | access | url | bias | why |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vakratsas_ambler_1999 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know? | Demetrios Vakratsas; Tim Ambler | 1999 | Peer-reviewed review paper | Publisher/JSTOR page; library access may be needed | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103 | Academic review; older but foundational; not platform-funded | Breaks the simplistic ad -> belief -> purchase model; maps cognition, affect, and experience effects. |
| sethuraman_tellis_briesch_2011 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | How Well Does Advertising Work? Generalizations from Meta-Analysis of Brand Advertising Elasticities | Raj Sethuraman; Gerard J. Tellis; Richard A. Briesch | 2011 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Publisher page; author copies/mirrors may exist | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1509/jmkr.48.3.457 | Academic meta-analysis; publication and method heterogeneity still matter | Gives empirical benchmarks for how much advertising tends to move sales; prevents magical thinking. |
| korkames_stanley_stremersch_2025 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | Meta-analysis of Advertising Effectiveness: New Insights from Improved Bias Corrections | Joseph Korkames; T. D. Stanley; Stefan Stremersch | 2025/2026 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis / bias correction | Open PDF from author university page | https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf | Academic; explicitly focused on correcting publication/misspecification/aggregation bias | Important antidote to overconfident marketing claims; focuses on how bias inflates measured ad effects. |
| rosengren_eisend_koslow_dahlen_2020 | Advertising effectiveness | High | A Meta-Analysis of When and How Advertising Creativity Works | Sara Rosengren; Martin Eisend; Scott Koslow; Micael Dahlen | 2020 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Publisher page and open author PDFs available | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0022242920929288 | Academic; creativity effects depend heavily on measurement and context | Teaches when creative advertising changes responses and when it does not. |

This paper teaches that distinction. |

| edelman_brand_trust_2025 | Market/channel context | Useful/current | 2025 Edelman Trust Barometer Special Report: Brand Trust, From We to Me | Edelman | 2025 | Industry trust report | Open PDF | https://www.edelman.com/sites/g/files/aatuss191/files/2025-06/2025%20Edelman%20Trust%20Barometer%20Special%20Report%20Brand%20Trust%2C%20From%20We%20to%20Me_FINAL_0.pdf | PR firm; trust framing can serve client interests | Useful signal on consumer trust, brand expectations, and skepticism. |
| nielsen_trust_sell_sheet_2021 | Market/channel context | Useful | Trust in Advertising Study | Nielsen | 2021 | Industry research sell sheet | Open PDF | https://www.nielsen.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2022/02/2021-Nielsen-Trust-In-Advertising-U.S.-sell-sheet.pdf | Research product sell sheet; limited detail | Quick view of relative trust channels and message types. |
| grewal_satornino_davenport_guha_2025 | Generative AI in marketing | Essential | How generative AI Is shaping the future of marketing | Dhruv Grewal; Cinthia B. Satornino; Thomas Davenport; Abhijit Guha | 2025 | Peer-reviewed conceptual/review article | Springer page; open/full text may vary | https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01064-3 | Academic; broad strategy lens; not a tactical implementation manual | High-level map of how GenAI changes marketing research, strategy, actions, customer experience, and firm processes. |
| cillo_rubera_2025 | Generative AI in marketing | High | Generative AI in innovation and marketing processes | Paola Cillo; Gaia Rubera | 2025 | Peer-reviewed roadmap/research opportunities | Springer page | https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01044-7 | Academic; opportunity framing | Useful for seeing GenAI as part of product, innovation, and marketing processes rather than copy generation alone. |
| yang_2025_transforming_advertising_gai | Generative AI in advertising practice | High | Transforming advertising in the age of generative AI | J. Yang | 2025 | Peer-reviewed exploratory study | Taylor & Francis page | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2025.2604910 | Qualitative/exploratory; not causal performance evidence | Shows how advertising practitioners integrate GenAI into creative and production workflows. |
| reisenbichler_reutterer_schweidel_dan_2022 | AI content marketing | Essential | Frontiers: Supporting Content Marketing with Natural Language Generation | Martin Reisenbichler; Thomas Reutterer; David A. Schweidel; Daniel Dan | 2022 | Peer-reviewed Marketing Science article | Open author PDF | https://www.reutterer.com/papers/reisenbichler%26reutterer%26schweidel%26dan_2022.pdf | Academic; pre-ChatGPT NLG context but still methodologically useful | Grounds AI content use in semi-automated content marketing and SEO rather than vague productivity claims. |
| brand_israel_ngwe_2023_llm_market_research | AI market research | Essential | Using LLMs for Market Research | James Brand; Ayelet Israeli; Donald Ngwe | 2023 | Harvard Business School working paper | Open PDF | https://www.hbs.edu/ris/Publication%20Files/23-062_6bfe7a5b-3ed9-4afd-a4c1-c91b60dd82e5.pdf | Academic working paper; methods still developing | Shows how LLMs may be used to simulate or augment market research, while requiring careful validation against real human data. |
| wang_2024_llm_market_research_arxiv | AI market research | High | Large Language Models for Market Research: A Data Augmentation Approach | M. Wang et al. | 2024 | arXiv research paper | Open arXiv | https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19363 | Preprint; not final peer-reviewed; technical-statistical focus | Important warning: LLM-generated responses can help, but must be debiased/anchored with real human data. |
| aggarwal_2023_geo | AI search / GEO | Essential | GEO: Generative Engine Optimization | P. Aggarwal et al. | 2023/2024 | arXiv / research paper | Open PDF | https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.09735 | Early research; engines and products evolve quickly | Introduces the idea that visibility in generative answer engines requires different tactics from classic SEO. |
| chen_wang_chen_koudas_2025_geo_ai_search | AI search / GEO | Essential | Generative Engine Optimization: How to Dominate AI Search | Mahe Chen; Xiaoxuan Wang; Kaiwen Chen; Nick Koudas | 2025 | arXiv empirical analysis | Open arXiv | https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.08919 | Preprint; commercial relevance high but findings may shift as engines update | Compares AI search and traditional search; emphasizes earned media, source authority, freshness, and machine-readable justification. |
| nist_genai_profile_2024 | AI governance and risk | Essential | Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile | NIST | 2024 | Government risk management framework | Open PDF | https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf | Government/standards perspective; not marketing-specific | Core risk-management source for GenAI use: validity, privacy, security, transparency, and governance. |
| nist_ai_rmf | AI governance and risk | Essential | NIST AI Risk Management Framework | NIST | 2023/2024 | Government standards framework | Open web page and PDFs | https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework | Government/standards perspective | Use as your baseline for trustworthy AI management across design, deployment, monitoring, and governance. |
| owasp_llm_top_10_2025 | AI security | Essential | OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications | OWASP GenAI Security Project | 2025 | Security guidance | Open web page and PDF | https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/ | Security-focused; conservative by design | Needed if building AI chatbots, sales agents, support agents, website agents, or internal marketing automations. |
| stanford_ai_index_2025 | AI market context | Essential | The 2025 AI Index Report | Stanford HAI | 2025 | Annual AI index report | Open web page/PDF | https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report | Academic/institutional; broad AI scope, not marketing-specific | Current macro baseline for AI adoption, investment, capabilities, policy, and public perception. |
| mckinsey_state_ai_2025 | AI adoption / business | High | The State of AI: Global Survey 2025 | McKinsey & Company / QuantumBlack | 2025 | Consultancy survey/report | Open article/PDF | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai | Consultancy; useful signal but incentive to frame AI transformation opportunities | Current business adoption signal; useful for seeing which functions use AI and how firms report value. |
| mit_nanda_genai_divide_2025 | AI adoption / business | High | The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025 | MIT Project NANDA | 2025 | Research report | Open PDF mirror | https://mlq.ai/media/quarterly_decks/v0.1_State_of_AI_in_Business_2025_Report.pdf | Preliminary findings; methodology should be read carefully | Counterweight to hype: argues many GenAI projects fail because they are not integrated into workflows. |
| ftc_operation_ai_comply_2024 | AI advertising law and ethics | Essential | FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes | Federal Trade Commission | 2024 | Regulator enforcement release | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes | Regulator perspective; enforcement-focused | Essential warning against overclaiming AI capabilities, earnings outcomes, fake reviews, and AI-washing. |
| ftc_ai_industry_page | AI advertising law and ethics | High | FTC Artificial Intelligence Industry Page | Federal Trade Commission | Current | Regulator hub | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence | Regulator perspective | Use to monitor AI enforcement trends around deception, data use, endorsements, and consumer harm. |
| ftc_truth_advertising | Advertising law and ethics | Essential | Truth in Advertising | Federal Trade Commission | Current | Regulator guidance hub | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/topics/truth-advertising | Regulator perspective | Base rule: advertising claims must be truthful, not misleading, and substantiated where appropriate. |
| oecd_ai_principles | AI governance and risk | High | OECD AI Principles | OECD | 2019/2024 update | International policy principles | Open web page | https://oecd.ai/en/ai-principles | Policy lens; high-level | Good global baseline for trustworthy AI: human rights, transparency, robustness, accountability. |
| eu_ai_act_framework | AI regulation | High | EU AI Act regulatory framework | European Commission | 2024-2026 | Regulatory framework page | Open web page | https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai | EU regulatory perspective | Required if marketing or AI products reach EU users; useful baseline for risk-based AI governance. |
| eu_gpai_code_practice | AI regulation | Useful/current | General-Purpose AI Code of Practice | European Commission | 2025 | Regulatory code of practice | Open web page and downloadable chapters | https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/contents-code-gpai | EU regulatory perspective; provider-focused | Useful for understanding transparency, copyright, and safety expectations around general-purpose AI models. |
| google_ads_ai_overviews | AI search / ads | High | About ads and AI Overviews | Google Ads Help | Current | Platform documentation | Open web page | https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16297775?hl=en | Platform-owned; describes Google’s product framing | Source for how ads are integrated into AI Overviews and how AI search changes ad surfaces. |
| google_marketing_live_2025 | AI search / ads | Useful/current | Google Marketing Live 2025: News and Updates | Google | 2025 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-marketing-live-2025/ | Platform marketing announcement; product-promotion bias | Tracks current AI ad products, AI Mode/AI Overviews advertising, and campaign automation direction. |
| openai_instant_checkout_2025 | Agentic commerce | High | Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol | OpenAI | 2025 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://openai.com/index/buy-it-in-chatgpt/ | Platform-owned; product-promotion bias | Signals the movement from AI-assisted discovery to AI-mediated checkout and agentic commerce. |
| openai_product_discovery_2026 | Agentic commerce | High/current | Powering Product Discovery in ChatGPT | OpenAI | 2026 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/ | Platform-owned; product-promotion bias | Current signal for AI-native product discovery, comparison, and shopping behavior. |
| mckinsey_agentic_commerce_2025 | Agentic commerce | High | The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants | McKinsey & Company | 2025 | Consultancy report | Open web page | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-agentic-commerce-opportunity-how-ai-agents-are-ushering-in-a-new-era-for-consumers-and-merchants | Consultancy; opportunity framing | Useful for thinking beyond ads: buyer agents, merchant data, checkout, product feeds, and trust. |
| trustradius_b2b_ai_2025 | B2B software buying / AI | High/current | B2B Tech Buying in the Age of AI | TrustRadius | 2025 | Vendor/review-platform buyer report | Open PDF | https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-2025-Buyer-Research-Report-Top-10-Takeaways.pdf | Review platform; commercially biased, but useful buyer-behavior signal | Highlights AI Overviews, fact-checking, prior experience, pricing transparency, and the trust gap in B2B tech buying. |
| gartner_b2b_rep_free_2026 | B2B software buying / AI | High/current | Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience | Gartner | 2026 | Analyst press release | Open web page | https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience | Analyst firm; press release is summarized, not full methodology | Reinforces self-serve/software-buyer trend and the need to build AI-assisted buyer support instead of relying only on reps. |
| iab_genai_ad_playbook_2025 | Generative AI advertising practice | Useful/current | Generative AI Playbook for Advertising | IAB | 2025 | Industry playbook | Open PDF | https://www.iab.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/IAB_GenerativeAIPlaybook_January_26.pdf | Industry association; practice-heavy, less neutral than academic sources | Operational view of GenAI use cases, campaign optimization, production, creative, and governance. |
| doshi_hauser_2024_creativity | AI creativity | High | Generative AI enhances individual creativity but reduces the collective diversity of novel content | Anil R. Doshi; Oliver P. Hauser | 2024 | Peer-reviewed Science Advances article | Publisher page | https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adn5290 | Specific creative-writing context; generalize carefully | Important caution: AI can improve average output but flatten diversity. This matters for ads and brand differentiation. |
| kirk_givi_2025_ai_authorship | AI disclosure / trust | High | The AI-authorship effect: Understanding authenticity, moral disgust, and consumer responses to AI-generated marketing communications | C. P. Kirk; J. Givi | 2025 | Peer-reviewed Journal of Business Research article | Abstract/index page | https://ideas.repec.org/a/eee/jbrese/v186y2025ics0148296324004880.html | Experimental context; emotional communications may not generalize to all software ads | Shows that AI authorship can reduce authenticity, positive word of mouth, and loyalty for some emotional communications. |
| schilke_2025_transparency_dilemma | AI disclosure / trust | High | The transparency dilemma: How AI disclosure erodes trust | Oliver Schilke et al. | 2025 | Peer-reviewed article | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0749597825000172 | Context dependent; transparency can be legally/ethically necessary even if trust penalty exists | Nuanced source: disclosure can reduce trust, so transparency strategy must be handled carefully, not mechanically. |
| baek_2024_ai_disclosure_prosocial_ads | AI disclosure / trust | Useful | Effect of disclosing AI-generated content on prosocial advertising | T. H. Baek et al. | 2024 | Peer-reviewed advertising study | Taylor & Francis page | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2024.2401319 | Prosocial advertising context; not directly SaaS | Good example of how disclosure effects are context-sensitive and can influence attitudes/intentions. |
| chen_2024_ai_ad_appeals | AI advertising effectiveness | Useful | Appeal types, self-efficacy and AI's social role | Y. Chen et al. | 2024 | Peer-reviewed experimental article | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0148296324003710 | Experimental; limited contexts | Useful for choosing whether AI-created ads should use practical/agentic vs warm/communal appeals. |
| madathil_2025_human_ai_ads | AI advertising effectiveness | Useful | Generative AI advertisements and Human–AI collaboration | J. C. Madathil et al. | 2025 | Peer-reviewed advertising study | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698925001602 | Specific to video ads and human-AI cues | Useful for thinking about when human involvement should remain visible to protect perceived humanity and reduce anxiety. |
| exner_ai_in_disguise_2025 | AI advertising effectiveness | High | How AI-generated ads' visual cues shape consumer behavior | Y. Exner et al. | 2025 | Research paper / quasi-experimental advertising data | Open PDF | https://www.columbia.edu/~on2110/Papers/AI_in_disguise.pdf | Specific dataset and ad context; quasi-experimental assumptions matter | Useful because it links AI-generated visual cues to real ad behavior rather than opinions alone. |
| collaborating_ai_agents_ads_2025 | Human-AI collaboration | High | Collaborating with AI Agents: A Field Experiment on Ad Creation | Research authors via arXiv | 2025 | arXiv/field experiment | Open arXiv HTML | https://arxiv.org/html/2503.18238v2 | Preprint; evaluate methods carefully | Useful field evidence on human-AI collaboration in ad creation, productivity, and output quality. |
| fang_2025_genai_firm_productivity | AI productivity / workflows | High | Generative AI and Firm Productivity: Field Experiments in Online Retail | L. Fang et al. | 2025 | arXiv/field experiment | Open arXiv HTML / PDF | https://arxiv.org/html/2510.12049v1 | Preprint; online-retail setting; productivity != brand trust | Shows GenAI value emerges from embedded workflows, not isolated prompts. |
| adobe_b2b_ai_digital_trends_2025 | B2B AI marketing practice | Useful/current | 2025 AI and Digital Trends in B2B Journeys | Adobe | 2025 | Vendor report | Report page/gated in places | https://business.adobe.com/resources/reports/b2b-marketing-digital-trends.html | Vendor; promotes enterprise marketing software worldview | Useful market signal for AI-driven B2B journeys, content effectiveness, and personalization. |
| consumer_chatbot_acceptance_2025 | AI chatbots / conversational commerce | Useful | Consumer Acceptance of Conversational Bots: Systematic Literature Review | O. H. Fares et al. | 2025/2026 | Systematic literature review | Publisher page | https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/cb.70130 | Review article; customer-service/chatbot context varies | Useful for designing AI chat interfaces that buyers can trust and use rather than resent. |
